授業目的: International trade is an important foundation of economic development for any country. Therefore, many regional economic integration has been formed and deepened around the world. To understand the current and the future global economy, it is necessary to understand why economic integration has developed in the world, how it has deepened, and what challenges it poses. This course aims to provide students with analytical frameworks to understand various trade issues occurring in the real international economy. Based on basic trade theories, students will develop an understanding of various trade-related issues and the ability to apply it to policy discussions.
到達目標: The learning objectives of this course are the following three issues:1) to gain the basicknowledge of international trade theories;2) to acquire the knowledge of the latest academic research on major topics related to international trade;3) to deepen theunderstanding of the background and impact of current trade topics such as the deepening of regional economic integration, global value chains and digital servicetrade. Students are expected to be able to apply the knowledge acquired in this course to constructive policy discussions on the current globalized economy.
特記事項: 【合併】International Economic Integration　（経営戦略研究科）／International Economic Integration（国際学研究科前期）
授業の概要・背景:
This course consists of five sections. 
1) Introduction; 
2) basic trade theories; 
3) tradepolicy; 
4) trade and economic development; 
5) current trade topics. 
Each session will include lectures on theory, empirical issues, and analytical tools and data. In addition, students learn how to apply theory and empirical research to international trade policies through discussions and presentations in group works.
授業方法: Face to face class format.Lectures (60%) , Hands on exercises (30%) & Presentation (10%)

第1回: Introduction: overview of world trade & global economy
第2回: Introduction to trade theory: comparative advantage, trade patterns and benefit of trade
第3回: International trade theory 1: Classical trade model (the Ricardian Model)
第4回: International trade theory 2: Framework for Neo-classical model
第5回: International trade theory 3: Neo-classical Model (the Specific Factor Model)
第6回: International trade theory 4: Neo-classical Model (the Heckscher-Ohlin Model)
第7回: Short presentation session using winter break assignment
第8回: International trade theory 6: Modern Trade Theories (Economies of scale, location of production and Firms in the global economy)
第9回: Multinational enterpirses and Foregin Direct Investment
第12回: Research papers and reports to be distributed in advance
第13回: United Nations, Trade andDevelopment Report; etc.
第14回: Digital service trade and current issues in international trade
United Nations, Trade andDevelopment Report; etc.

教科書/Required texts0: Paul R. Krugman, Maurice Obstfeld , Marc Melitz (2022) "International Economics:Theory and Policy", Global Edition, 12th, Pearson
参考文献・資料/Reference books0: 1) Kenneth A. Reinert (2021) "An Introduction to International Economics: New Perspectives on the World Economy" Cambridge University Press, 2nd Edition2) Various reports published by international institutions such as World Bank and WTO3) Research papers to be related to international economics, to be suggested in the course.
課題等へのフィードバック方法Methods for Providing Feed: The feedback to quizzes and assignments are given via LUNA. Example answers to the end-term exam will be available via LUNA after completion.
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading1: A written exam will be held in the final session (Session 15).
成績評価Grading2: 授業外学修課題／Assignment, homework required outside class (06)
Quizzes to review lecture content in each session.
成績評価Grading3: プレゼンテーション・発表／Presentation (07)
Short presentation based on the winter holiday assignment

name: 【630】International Economic Integration／International Economic Integration
